title: README from Project Info
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_readme.py` that reads `project.json` and generates `README.md` with sections: project name as H1, a Description section, an Installation section, a Usage section, and a License section. Each section should use the corresponding field from the JSON.

input.project.json:
{
  "name": "fastcache",
  "description": "A lightweight in-memory caching library for Python applications.",
  "installation": "pip install fastcache",
  "usage": "from fastcache import Cache\ncache = Cache(ttl=60)\ncache.set('key', 'value')\nprint(cache.get('key'))",
  "license": "MIT"
}

title: Changelog from Commits
prompt: Write a Python script `changelog.py` that reads `commits.txt` (one commit per line, format: `YYYY-MM-DD TYPE message`) and generates `CHANGELOG.md`. Group entries under H2 headings by TYPE (feat, fix, docs, refactor). Each entry should be a bullet point with the date and message.

input.commits.txt:
2025-03-01 feat add user authentication
2025-03-02 fix resolve login timeout
2025-03-03 docs update API reference
2025-03-04 feat implement search endpoint
2025-03-05 fix handle null email field
2025-03-06 refactor extract validation logic
2025-03-07 feat add rate limiting
2025-03-08 docs add contributing guide
2025-03-09 fix correct timezone handling
2025-03-10 refactor simplify database queries

title: Gitignore Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_gitignore.py` that reads `stack.txt` (one technology per line) and generates a `.gitignore` file with common ignore patterns for each technology. Support at least: python, node, java, rust. Write the output to `output.gitignore`.

title: Meeting Notes Formatter
prompt: Write a Python script `format_notes.py` that reads `raw_notes.txt` and produces `meeting_notes.md`. Lines starting with `@` are attendees, lines starting with `>` are decisions, lines starting with `!` are action items, and other lines are discussion points. Output should have sections: Attendees (bulleted list), Discussion, Decisions, and Action Items.

input.raw_notes.txt:
@Alice
@Bob
@Carol
Discussed Q2 roadmap priorities
Need to finalize budget by Friday
>Approved migration to new CI/CD pipeline
>Agreed to hire two more engineers
!Alice: Draft migration plan by Monday
!Bob: Review budget proposal
!Carol: Schedule interviews for next week
Reviewed sprint velocity trends

title: License File Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_license.py` that reads `license_info.json` and generates `LICENSE` file. The JSON contains `type` (MIT or Apache-2.0), `year`, and `holder`. Generate the standard license text with the correct year and holder substituted.

title: Plain Text to Bullet List
prompt: Write a Python script `bulletify.py` that reads `items.txt` (one item per line, possibly with leading/trailing whitespace) and writes `items.md` with each non-empty line as a markdown bullet point (`- item`). Add an H1 title `# Items` at the top. Empty lines should be skipped.

input.items.txt:
Apples  
Bananas

  Carrots
Dragonfruit
   
Elderberries
Figs
Grapes

title: Email Draft from Bullet Points
prompt: Write a Python script `draft_email.py` that reads `brief.json` (with fields: `to`, `from`, `subject`, `points` array) and writes a professional email to `email.txt`. The email should have a greeting, expand each bullet point into a paragraph sentence, and close with a sign-off using the sender name.

input.brief.json:
{
  "to": "Engineering Team",
  "from": "Sarah Chen",
  "subject": "Q3 Planning Update",
  "points": [
    "Sprint velocity increased 15% this quarter",
    "New CI pipeline reduced build times by 40%",
    "Three open positions approved for backend team",
    "Architecture review scheduled for next Friday"
  ]
}

title: INI Config Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_config.py` that reads `settings.json` and generates `app.ini` in INI format. The JSON has section names as top-level keys, each mapping to a dict of key-value pairs. Use Python's `configparser` module to write the INI file.

title: CSV to Markdown Table
prompt: Write a Python script `csv2md.py` that reads `data.csv` and converts it to a markdown table in `table.md`. The first row of the CSV is the header. Include proper markdown table separators (`|---|---|`).

input.data.csv:
Name,Language,Stars,License
FastAPI,Python,65000,MIT
Express,JavaScript,61000,MIT
Gin,Go,72000,MIT
Actix,Rust,18000,Apache-2.0
Spring,Java,70000,Apache-2.0

title: YAML Config from Requirements
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_yaml.py` that reads `requirements.txt` (lines of `key=value` with optional section headers in `[brackets]`) and outputs `config.yaml` in valid YAML format. Section headers become top-level keys, and key=value pairs become nested under the current section.

input.requirements.txt:
[app]
name=myservice
version=1.0.0
port=3000
[database]
engine=postgresql
host=db.example.com
port=5432
[cache]
backend=redis
ttl=300

title: Text Wrapper Utility
prompt: Write a Python script `wrap_text.py` that reads `input.txt` and writes `wrapped.txt` with all lines wrapped to a maximum of 72 characters. Use Python's `textwrap` module. Preserve paragraph breaks (empty lines). Do not break words in the middle.

input.input.txt:
This is a very long line that should be wrapped because it exceeds the seventy-two character limit that we have set for our output document formatting requirements.

This is a second paragraph that is also quite long and needs to be properly wrapped to fit within the specified character width limit of seventy-two characters per line.

Short line.

checks.cmd: python -c "lines=open('wrapped.txt').readlines(); assert all(len(l.rstrip()) <= 72 for l in lines), 'Line exceeds 72 chars'"

title: Sorted Glossary Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `glossary.py` that reads `terms.txt` (format: `TERM: definition` per line) and generates `glossary.md` with terms alphabetically sorted. Each term should be a bold markdown item (`**Term**`) followed by its definition. Add an H1 title `# Glossary`.

input.terms.txt:
REST: Representational State Transfer, an architectural style for APIs
API: Application Programming Interface
JSON: JavaScript Object Notation, a data interchange format
CRUD: Create, Read, Update, Delete operations
HTTP: Hypertext Transfer Protocol
ORM: Object-Relational Mapping
SDK: Software Development Kit
CI: Continuous Integration

checks.cmd: python -c "lines=[l for l in open('glossary.md').readlines() if l.startswith('**')]; terms=[l.split('**')[1] for l in lines]; assert terms==sorted(terms), 'Not sorted'"

title: Simple Template Filler
prompt: Write a Python script `fill_template.py` that reads `template.txt` (containing `${key}` placeholders) and `values.json` (key-value pairs) and writes `output.txt` with all placeholders replaced by their values.

input.template.txt:
Dear ${name},

Thank you for your order #${order_id}.
Your ${product} will be shipped to ${address}.
Estimated delivery: ${delivery_date}.

Best regards,
${company}
input.values.json:
{
  "name": "Alex Rivera",
  "order_id": "ORD-2025-1847",
  "product": "Mechanical Keyboard",
  "address": "123 Oak Street, Portland, OR",
  "delivery_date": "March 15, 2025",
  "company": "TechGear Inc."
}

title: CLI Usage Doc Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_usage.py` that reads `cli_spec.json` (with `name`, `description`, `commands` array each having `name`, `args`, `description`) and generates `USAGE.md` with the tool name as H1, description paragraph, and each command documented with its arguments and description.

title: Numbered List Extractor
prompt: Write a Python script `extract_steps.py` that reads `guide.txt` and extracts only lines that start with a number followed by a period (e.g., `1. Step one`). Write the extracted steps to `steps.md` with an H1 heading `# Steps` at the top, preserving the original numbering.

input.guide.txt:
Getting Started with Docker

Prerequisites: You need a Linux or Mac system.

1. Install Docker from the official website

Make sure to follow the OS-specific instructions.

2. Verify installation with docker --version
3. Pull your first image: docker pull hello-world

This downloads the test image.

4. Run the container: docker run hello-world
5. Check running containers: docker ps

Congratulations, you're all set!

title: Contact Card Formatter
prompt: Write a Python script `format_contacts.py` that reads `contacts.json` (an array of objects with `name`, `email`, `phone`, `role`) and generates `contacts.md` with each contact as an H3 heading (name) followed by role, email, and phone as bullet points.

title: Env File Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_env.py` that reads `env_spec.json` (object with keys as variable names, values as objects with `default` and `description`) and generates a `.env.example` file. Each entry should have a comment line with the description, followed by the variable set to its default value.

title: Decision Log Formatter
prompt: Write a Python script `decision_log.py` that reads `decisions.json` (array of objects with `date`, `title`, `context`, `decision`, `status`) and writes `decisions.md`. Each decision should be an H2 with the title, followed by metadata (date, status) and the context and decision as paragraphs.

title: Markdown Link Checker List
prompt: Write a Python script `extract_links.py` that reads `doc.md` and extracts all markdown links (format `[text](url)`). Write them to `links.txt` with one URL per line, deduplicated and sorted alphabetically.

input.doc.md:
# Resources

Check out [Python docs](https://docs.python.org) for reference.
Also see [MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org) for web APIs.

## Tools

- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com) is a great editor
- [GitHub](https://github.com) for version control
- [Python docs](https://docs.python.org) has tutorials too

## More

Visit [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com) for help.

title: Markdown Heading Hierarchy
prompt: Write a Python script `toc.py` that reads `document.md` and generates `toc.md` containing a table of contents. Extract all headings (lines starting with `#`), and output them as an indented list where H1 has no indent, H2 has 2-space indent, H3 has 4-space indent, etc. Format each as `- Heading Text`.

input.document.md:
# Project Overview

Some intro text.

## Architecture

Details here.

### Frontend

React-based SPA.

### Backend

Python FastAPI service.

## Deployment

### Docker

Containerized deployment.

### Kubernetes

Orchestration setup.

## Contributing

How to contribute.

title: API Documentation Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_api_docs.py` that reads `api.json` (array of endpoint objects with `method`, `path`, `description`, `params` array, `response` object) and generates `API.md`. Each endpoint gets an H2 heading with method and path, a description, a parameters table (name, type, required columns), and a response example in a fenced code block.

title: Multi-format Config Converter
prompt: Write a Python script `convert_config.py` that reads `config.json` and generates three output files: `config.ini` (INI format), `config.env` (KEY=value format with underscored flattened keys like `SECTION_KEY`), and `config.yaml` (YAML format). The JSON has nested section/key structure.

title: Technical Spec from Requirements
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_spec.py` that reads `requirements.json` (with `title`, `overview`, `functional_requirements` array, `non_functional_requirements` array, `constraints` array) and generates `SPEC.md`. Include sections: Title (H1), Overview, Functional Requirements (numbered list), Non-Functional Requirements (numbered list), Constraints (bulleted list), and a Revision History table with one row for today's date as initial version.

input.requirements.json:
{
  "title": "User Authentication Service",
  "overview": "A microservice handling user registration, login, and session management with JWT-based authentication.",
  "functional_requirements": [
    "Support email/password registration",
    "Implement JWT access and refresh tokens",
    "Provide password reset via email",
    "Support OAuth2 login with Google and GitHub",
    "Rate limit login attempts to 5 per minute"
  ],
  "non_functional_requirements": [
    "Response time under 200ms for auth endpoints",
    "Support 10000 concurrent sessions",
    "99.9% uptime SLA",
    "All passwords hashed with bcrypt"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Must use PostgreSQL for user storage",
    "Must be deployable as a Docker container",
    "API must follow OpenAPI 3.0 spec"
  ]
}

title: Release Notes Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `release_notes.py` that reads `changes.json` (with `version`, `date`, `highlights` string, `changes` array of objects with `type`, `scope`, `description`) and writes `RELEASE.md`. Group changes by type (Features, Bug Fixes, Breaking Changes, Performance). Include a summary block with version, date, and highlights at the top.

input.changes.json:
{
  "version": "2.3.0",
  "date": "2025-03-15",
  "highlights": "This release introduces real-time notifications and improves query performance by 3x.",
  "changes": [
    {"type": "feature", "scope": "notifications", "description": "Add WebSocket-based real-time notifications"},
    {"type": "feature", "scope": "api", "description": "New batch endpoint for bulk operations"},
    {"type": "bugfix", "scope": "auth", "description": "Fix token refresh race condition"},
    {"type": "bugfix", "scope": "database", "description": "Resolve connection pool exhaustion under load"},
    {"type": "performance", "scope": "queries", "description": "Optimize N+1 queries in user listing"},
    {"type": "breaking", "scope": "api", "description": "Rename /api/v1/users to /api/v2/accounts"},
    {"type": "feature", "scope": "search", "description": "Full-text search with relevance ranking"},
    {"type": "performance", "scope": "cache", "description": "Add Redis caching layer for hot queries"}
  ]
}

title: JSON Schema Documentation
prompt: Write a Python script `schema_docs.py` that reads `schema.json` (a JSON Schema object) and generates `SCHEMA.md` documenting each property. Include the object title as H1, description as intro paragraph, and a table with columns: Property, Type, Required, Description for each property.

title: Dockerfile Documentation
prompt: Write a Python script `doc_dockerfile.py` that reads `Dockerfile` and generates `DOCKER.md` documenting it. Extract the base image, all ENV variables, EXPOSE ports, and COPY/ADD instructions. Create sections: Base Image, Environment Variables (table with name and value), Exposed Ports, Build Steps (numbered list of each instruction), and a Quick Start section with docker build and run commands using the image name from the last ENV or a default.

input.Dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim

ENV APP_NAME=myservice
ENV APP_PORT=8000
ENV LOG_LEVEL=info
ENV WORKERS=4

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY src/ ./src/
COPY config/ ./config/

EXPOSE 8000
EXPOSE 9090

CMD ["python", "-m", "uvicorn", "src.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]

title: Markdown Resume Builder
prompt: Write a Python script `build_resume.py` that reads `resume_data.json` and generates `resume.md`. Include sections: Name (H1), Contact Info, Summary, Experience (with company, role, dates, and bullet-point achievements), Education, and Skills (grouped by category).

title: Git Commit Summarizer
prompt: Write a Python script `summarize_commits.py` that reads `git_log.txt` (one commit per line: `hash date author message`) and generates `summary.md` with: total commit count, date range, a table of commits per author, and a list of the 5 most recent commits. Group statistics in a Summary section and details in a Recent Activity section.

input.git_log.txt:
a1b2c3d 2025-03-01 alice Fix login redirect bug
e4f5g6h 2025-03-01 bob Add user search API
i7j8k9l 2025-03-02 alice Refactor auth middleware
m0n1o2p 2025-03-02 carol Update deployment docs
q3r4s5t 2025-03-03 bob Add pagination support
u6v7w8x 2025-03-03 alice Add rate limiting
y9z0a1b 2025-03-04 carol Fix CSS layout issue
c2d3e4f 2025-03-04 bob Optimize database queries
g5h6i7j 2025-03-05 alice Add export feature
k8l9m0n 2025-03-05 carol Write unit tests for auth
o1p2q3r 2025-03-06 bob Add webhook support
s4t5u6v 2025-03-06 alice Fix memory leak in cache

title: Error Code Reference
prompt: Write a Python script `error_ref.py` that reads `errors.json` (array of objects with `code`, `name`, `http_status`, `description`, `resolution`) and generates `ERRORS.md`. Include an H1 title, an overview count, and each error as an H3 with code and name, followed by HTTP status badge text, description, and a resolution tip in a blockquote.

input.errors.json:
[
  {"code": "E001", "name": "AuthenticationFailed", "http_status": 401, "description": "The provided credentials are invalid or expired.", "resolution": "Check your API key and ensure it has not been revoked."},
  {"code": "E002", "name": "ResourceNotFound", "http_status": 404, "description": "The requested resource does not exist.", "resolution": "Verify the resource ID and check that it has not been deleted."},
  {"code": "E003", "name": "RateLimitExceeded", "http_status": 429, "description": "Too many requests in the given time window.", "resolution": "Implement exponential backoff and respect Retry-After headers."},
  {"code": "E004", "name": "ValidationError", "http_status": 400, "description": "The request body contains invalid or missing fields.", "resolution": "Review the API documentation for required fields and data types."},
  {"code": "E005", "name": "InternalError", "http_status": 500, "description": "An unexpected server error occurred.", "resolution": "Retry the request. If the issue persists, contact support."}
]

title: Markdown Merge and Dedup
prompt: Write a Python script `merge_docs.py` that reads all `.md` files listed in `manifest.txt` (one filename per line), merges them into a single `merged.md` file. Deduplicate any sections with identical H2 headings (keep the first occurrence). Add a generated table of contents at the top listing all H2 sections as links.

input.part1.md:
## Installation

Run `pip install mylib`.

## Configuration

Set env variables as needed.
input.part2.md:
## Usage

Import and call `mylib.run()`.

## Installation

Duplicate section to be removed.
input.part3.md:
## API Reference

### get_data()

Returns the current dataset.

## Troubleshooting

Check logs at `/var/log/mylib.log`.

title: Interview Questions Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_questions.py` that reads `topics.json` (array of objects with `topic`, `level`, `count`) and generates `questions.md`. For each topic, generate the specified count of interview-style questions appropriate for the level (junior/mid/senior). Use H2 for each topic, H3 for levels, and numbered lists for questions. Output must have at least one question per entry.

title: TOML Config Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_pyproject.py` that reads `project_meta.json` (with `name`, `version`, `description`, `authors` array, `python_requires`, `dependencies` array, `dev_dependencies` array) and generates a valid `pyproject.toml` file with `[project]` and `[project.optional-dependencies]` sections. Do NOT use any third-party TOML library; format the TOML manually.

input.project_meta.json:
{
  "name": "dataflow",
  "version": "0.5.2",
  "description": "A data pipeline orchestration framework",
  "authors": ["Alice Chen <alice@example.com>", "Bob Park <bob@example.com>"],
  "python_requires": ">=3.9",
  "dependencies": ["click>=8.0", "pydantic>=2.0", "httpx>=0.24"],
  "dev_dependencies": ["pytest>=7.0", "black>=23.0", "mypy>=1.0"]
}

title: Structured Diff Report
prompt: Write a Python script `diff_report.py` that reads `old_config.json` and `new_config.json` and generates `diff_report.md` listing all changes. Categorize changes as Added, Removed, or Modified. For modified values show old and new values. Use a table for each category.

title: FAQ Document Builder
prompt: Write a Python script `build_faq.py` that reads `qa_pairs.json` (array of objects with `category`, `question`, `answer`) and generates `FAQ.md`. Group questions by category under H2 headings. Each Q&A should use the question as an H3 and the answer as body text. Add a category index at the top with links (using markdown anchors).

input.qa_pairs.json:
[
  {"category": "Account", "question": "How do I reset my password?", "answer": "Click the Forgot Password link on the login page and follow the email instructions."},
  {"category": "Account", "question": "Can I change my username?", "answer": "Yes, go to Settings > Profile and update your username."},
  {"category": "Billing", "question": "What payment methods are accepted?", "answer": "We accept Visa, Mastercard, and PayPal."},
  {"category": "Billing", "question": "How do I cancel my subscription?", "answer": "Navigate to Settings > Subscription and click Cancel Plan."},
  {"category": "Technical", "question": "What browsers are supported?", "answer": "Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge (latest two versions)."},
  {"category": "Technical", "question": "Is there an API available?", "answer": "Yes, see our API documentation at docs.example.com/api."}
]

title: Multi-language i18n File Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_i18n.py` that reads `strings.json` (object mapping keys to objects with language codes as keys and translations as values) and generates one file per language: `en.json`, `es.json`, `fr.json`. Each output file should be a flat JSON object mapping keys to the translation for that language. Output files must be valid, pretty-printed JSON.

title: Dependency Report
prompt: Write a Python script `dep_report.py` that reads `dependencies.json` (array of objects with `name`, `version`, `license`, `latest_version`, `description`) and generates `DEPENDENCIES.md`. Include a summary table of all dependencies, flag outdated ones (where `version` != `latest_version`) in a separate section, and group by license type at the bottom.

input.dependencies.json:
[
  {"name": "requests", "version": "2.28.0", "license": "Apache-2.0", "latest_version": "2.31.0", "description": "HTTP library"},
  {"name": "click", "version": "8.1.7", "license": "BSD-3", "latest_version": "8.1.7", "description": "CLI toolkit"},
  {"name": "pydantic", "version": "2.0.0", "license": "MIT", "latest_version": "2.5.3", "description": "Data validation"},
  {"name": "sqlalchemy", "version": "2.0.23", "license": "MIT", "latest_version": "2.0.23", "description": "SQL toolkit and ORM"},
  {"name": "celery", "version": "5.3.1", "license": "BSD-3", "latest_version": "5.3.6", "description": "Distributed task queue"},
  {"name": "boto3", "version": "1.28.0", "license": "Apache-2.0", "latest_version": "1.34.0", "description": "AWS SDK"}
]

title: Markdown Slide Deck
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_slides.py` that reads `presentation.json` (with `title`, `author`, `slides` array of objects with `heading`, `bullets` array, optional `notes`) and generates `slides.md` in a Marp-compatible format. Use `---` as slide separators, include a YAML front matter with `marp: true` and `theme: default`, and add speaker notes under each slide using HTML comment syntax `<!-- notes -->` when present.

title: Code Comment Documentation Extractor
prompt: Write a Python script `extract_docs.py` that reads `source.py` and extracts all docstrings (triple-quoted strings after def/class lines) into `docs.md`. Each function/class should be an H3 with its name and signature, followed by the docstring as body text. Include an H1 title `# API Documentation` and count of documented items.

input.source.py:
class DataProcessor:
    """Handles data processing pipelines.
    
    Supports CSV, JSON, and Parquet formats.
    """
    
    def load(self, path, format='csv'):
        """Load data from a file.
        
        Args:
            path: File path to load from.
            format: Data format (csv, json, parquet).
        
        Returns:
            DataFrame with the loaded data.
        """
        pass
    
    def transform(self, operations):
        """Apply a list of transformations.
        
        Args:
            operations: List of operation dicts.
        
        Returns:
            Transformed dataset.
        """
        pass
    
    def export(self, path, format='csv'):
        """Export processed data to file.
        
        Args:
            path: Output file path.
            format: Export format.
        """
        pass

def validate_schema(data, schema):
    """Validate data against a JSON schema.
    
    Args:
        data: Input data dict.
        schema: JSON schema dict.
    
    Returns:
        True if valid, raises ValueError otherwise.
    """
    pass

title: Project Board from Tasks
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_board.py` that reads `tasks.json` (array of objects with `id`, `title`, `status`, `assignee`, `priority`) and generates `BOARD.md`. Group tasks into columns by status (todo, in_progress, done). Within each column, sort by priority (high > medium > low). Format as a table per status group showing ID, title, assignee, and priority.

input.tasks.json:
[
  {"id": "T-001", "title": "Set up CI pipeline", "status": "done", "assignee": "Alice", "priority": "high"},
  {"id": "T-002", "title": "Design database schema", "status": "in_progress", "assignee": "Bob", "priority": "high"},
  {"id": "T-003", "title": "Write API tests", "status": "todo", "assignee": "Carol", "priority": "medium"},
  {"id": "T-004", "title": "Implement auth flow", "status": "in_progress", "assignee": "Alice", "priority": "high"},
  {"id": "T-005", "title": "Create landing page", "status": "todo", "assignee": "Dave", "priority": "low"},
  {"id": "T-006", "title": "Set up monitoring", "status": "todo", "assignee": "Bob", "priority": "high"},
  {"id": "T-007", "title": "Write user docs", "status": "todo", "assignee": "Carol", "priority": "low"},
  {"id": "T-008", "title": "Deploy staging env", "status": "done", "assignee": "Dave", "priority": "medium"}
]

title: Markdown Footnote Injector
prompt: Write a Python script `add_footnotes.py` that reads `article.md` (containing inline references like `[ref:key]`) and `references.json` (mapping keys to citation strings). Replace each `[ref:key]` with a markdown footnote marker `[^key]` and append a footnote section at the bottom with all referenced citations. Write output to `article_final.md`.

input.article.md:
# The Rise of LLMs

Large language models have transformed NLP [ref:vaswani2017]. The introduction of
GPT demonstrated the power of unsupervised pretraining [ref:radford2018].

Recent work on instruction tuning [ref:ouyang2022] has made these models
more useful for everyday tasks. Scaling laws [ref:kaplan2020] suggest that
performance improves predictably with more compute.

## Conclusion

The field continues to evolve rapidly [ref:vaswani2017].

title: Architecture Decision Record Suite
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_adrs.py` that reads `decisions.json` (array of ADR objects with `id`, `title`, `date`, `status`, `context`, `decision`, `consequences`, `alternatives` array) and generates: (1) individual ADR files in an `adrs/` directory named `NNN-title-slug.md` (where NNN is zero-padded id), (2) an `adrs/INDEX.md` with a table linking to each ADR with id, title, date, and status. Each ADR file must follow the standard ADR template format with sections: Title (H1), Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, and Alternatives Considered.

title: Runbook Generator from Incidents
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_runbook.py` that reads `incidents.json` (array of incident objects with `id`, `severity`, `title`, `symptoms` array, `root_cause`, `resolution_steps` array, `prevention`) and generates `RUNBOOK.md`. Organize by severity (critical first). Each incident is an H2 with title, includes symptom checklist (checkbox markdown), numbered resolution steps, root cause in a blockquote, and prevention tip. Include a quick-lookup table at the top mapping symptom keywords to incident IDs.

title: Full Getting-Started Guide
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_guide.py` that reads `project.json` (with `name`, `language`, `package_manager`, `prerequisites` array, `setup_steps` array of {step, command, explanation}, `examples` array of {title, code, output}, `faq` array of {q, a}) and generates `GETTING_STARTED.md`. Include: Title, Prerequisites checklist, Setup (numbered steps with code blocks), Examples (titled code blocks with expected output), FAQ, and a Troubleshooting section synthesized from the FAQ. Add a table of contents at the top.

title: Multi-section Report from Survey Data
prompt: Write a Python script `survey_report.py` that reads `survey.json` (with `title`, `date`, `respondents` count, `questions` array of {text, type, responses}) and generates `report.md`. For multiple-choice questions (type='mc'), show a frequency table and the most popular answer. For rating questions (type='rating'), compute mean, median, min, max. For open-ended (type='open'), list unique responses as bullets. Include an Executive Summary section at the top with overall respondent count and key findings.

title: Codebase Documentation Index
prompt: Write a Python script `doc_index.py` that reads `modules.json` (array of module objects with `name`, `path`, `description`, `functions` array of {name, args, returns, docstring}, `dependencies` array) and generates: (1) `docs/index.md` with a module overview table and dependency graph in Mermaid syntax, (2) one `docs/MODULE_NAME.md` per module with function documentation including signature, return type, and docstring. Each module doc should have a table of contents of its functions.

title: Compliance Checklist Report
prompt: Write a Python script `compliance_report.py` that reads `audit.json` (with `standard`, `audit_date`, `auditor`, `categories` array of {name, requirements array of {id, description, status, evidence, notes}}) and generates `COMPLIANCE.md`. Include: (1) Executive summary with pass/fail/partial counts and an overall compliance percentage, (2) per-category sections with requirement tables (id, description, status with emoji checkmarks, evidence), (3) a Risk Items section listing all non-passing requirements sorted by category, (4) a sign-off section at the bottom with auditor and date.

title: Full Static Site Generator
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_site.py` that reads `site.json` (with `title`, `author`, `pages` array of {slug, title, content, nav_order}) and generates a `site/` directory containing: (1) one HTML file per page (`slug.html`) with a basic HTML template including header with site title, navigation links to all pages (sorted by nav_order), the page content rendered in a `<main>` tag, and a footer with author name, (2) an `index.html` that redirects to the first page by nav_order, (3) a `style.css` with basic readable styling. All HTML must be valid.

title: Test Matrix Documentation
prompt: Write a Python script `test_matrix.py` that reads `test_plan.json` (with `project`, `environments` array, `browsers` array, `features` array of {name, test_cases array of {id, description, priority, automated}}) and generates `TEST_MATRIX.md`. Include: (1) a cross-reference matrix table (features x environments) showing test coverage, (2) a browser compatibility matrix, (3) detailed test cases per feature with priority badges, (4) summary statistics (total cases, automated percentage, per-priority breakdown), (5) a coverage heatmap description showing which feature-environment combinations need attention.

title: Migration Guide from Diff
prompt: Write a Python script `migration_guide.py` that reads `api_v1.json` and `api_v2.json` (each an array of endpoint objects with `method`, `path`, `params`, `response_fields`) and generates `MIGRATION.md`. The guide should: (1) detect removed endpoints, (2) detect new endpoints, (3) detect changed endpoints (different params or response_fields), (4) for each change provide a Before/After code example showing the old and new request format, (5) include a migration checklist at the bottom. Sort by change severity (removed > changed > added).

title: Documentation Site with Cross-references
prompt: Write a Python script `gen_docs_site.py` that reads `knowledge_base.json` (array of article objects with `id`, `title`, `category`, `content`, `related_ids` array, `tags` array) and generates a `kb/` directory with: (1) one markdown file per article named `id.md` where related_ids are rendered as working relative links to other article files, (2) `kb/index.md` with articles grouped by category, (3) `kb/tags.md` listing all unique tags and under each tag the articles that have it, (4) each article file should include breadcrumb navigation (Home > Category > Article title). Verify all cross-reference links point to existing files.

input.knowledge_base.json:
[
  {"id": "kb-001", "title": "Getting Started", "category": "Basics", "content": "Welcome to the platform. Start here to learn the fundamentals.", "related_ids": ["kb-002", "kb-003"], "tags": ["beginner", "overview"]},
  {"id": "kb-002", "title": "Authentication Setup", "category": "Basics", "content": "Configure SSO or username/password auth for your organization.", "related_ids": ["kb-001", "kb-004"], "tags": ["auth", "beginner", "security"]},
  {"id": "kb-003", "title": "API Quick Start", "category": "API", "content": "Make your first API call in under 5 minutes.", "related_ids": ["kb-001", "kb-005"], "tags": ["api", "beginner"]},
  {"id": "kb-004", "title": "Advanced Security", "category": "Security", "content": "Configure IP whitelisting, audit logs, and encryption at rest.", "related_ids": ["kb-002"], "tags": ["security", "advanced"]},
  {"id": "kb-005", "title": "Webhook Integration", "category": "API", "content": "Set up webhooks to receive real-time event notifications.", "related_ids": ["kb-003", "kb-006"], "tags": ["api", "integration", "advanced"]},
  {"id": "kb-006", "title": "Troubleshooting Guide", "category": "Support", "content": "Common issues and their resolutions.", "related_ids": ["kb-001", "kb-005"], "tags": ["support", "troubleshooting"]}
]
